activities_projects.title: Investment in Human vs. Physical Capital
activities_projects.objective: Compare the returns on investment for different types of capital
steps: Research how a country like Japan became developed despite lacking natural resources
Create a comparison chart between 'Physical Capital' (machines) and 'Human Capital' (people)
Explain why human capital is considered 'superior' to other resources like land
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes why human resource is the ultimate factor of production
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison and historical context (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Virtuous vs. Vicious Cycle Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the socio-economic patterns of family development
steps: Map out a 'Virtuous Cycle' of an educated parent investing in a child's health and education
Contrast it with a 'Vicious Cycle' where illiterate parents lack the means to invest
Identify two government interventions that can break a vicious cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes how education levels perpetuate or break poverty
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and logic of the cycle diagrams (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: National Income and Gendered Labour
activities_projects.objective: Critically examine why certain labor is excluded from economic statistics
steps: Read the section on women's work in the household
Compare the 'Economic Value' of a domestic worker (paid) versus a housewife (unpaid)
Discuss how education and skill levels determine the 'Gender Pay Gap' in the market
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the determinants of earnings in the labor market
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical thinking on gender economics (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Education Infrastructure Audit
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the effectiveness of government education policies
steps: Analyze the 'Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan' and 'Bridge Courses' mentioned in the text
List three reasons why the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) might differ between primary and higher education
Suggest how 'Vocationalisation' of education can reduce unemployment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critiques the quality and reach of the Indian education system
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of suggestions and policy analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Health Indicators Comparative Study
activities_projects.objective: Interpret health data to understand population quality
steps: Use textbook data to compare 'Infant Mortality Rate' (IMR) and 'Life Expectancy' over decades
Plot a line graph showing the decline in IMR from 1951 to recent years
Explain how a high IMR reflects the poor quality of a country's human resource
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Interprets health statistics as economic indicators
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the graph and data interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Disguised Unemployment Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand marginal productivity in the agricultural sector
steps: Assume a farm produces 1000kg of wheat with 5 workers
If 3 more family members join and the production stays at 1000kg, calculate the 'Marginal Productivity' of the new workers
Explain why this is called 'Disguised' and its impact on family income
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the concept of marginal productivity to unemployment
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct mathematical logic and definition (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Paradox of Educated Unemployment
activities_projects.objective: Investigate why surplus manpower and manpower shortage coexist
steps: Analyze the statement: 'Unemployment among graduates is higher than matriculates'
Interview a local employer about the 'Skill Gap' they see in applicants
Write a report on why India has a surplus of graduates but a shortage of skilled technicians
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the structural issues in the urban labor market
activities_projects.evaluation: Insights from the report on the skill-education mismatch (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Employment Scenarios Across Sectors
activities_projects.objective: Trace the shift of labor from Primary to Secondary and Tertiary sectors
steps: Identify which sector (Primary, Secondary, Tertiary) is the largest employer in India
Explain the growth of the 'Tertiary Sector' in terms of new services like IT and Biotech
Analyze why surplus labor is moving away from agriculture
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the sectoral shift in the Indian economy
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining sectoral transitions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Impact of Unemployment on Society
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the social and psychological costs of being unemployed
steps: List three ways unemployment leads to 'Wastage of Manpower Resource'
Discuss how unemployment increases 'Economic Overload' on the working population
Create a poster showing the link between unemployment and social unrest/crime
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates unemployment as a social waste and a threat to growth
activities_projects.evaluation: Comprehensive list of impacts and creative poster (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Designing a Skill Development Program
activities_projects.objective: Apply chapter concepts to solve a community problem
steps: Identify a group of unemployed youth in a hypothetical village
Choose one vocational skill (e.g., Solar panel repair, Tailoring, Data entry)
Outline a 3-month 'Human Capital' investment plan including training and health support
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes education, health, and skill into a development plan
activities_projects.evaluation: Practicality and detail of the training plan (10 marks)
